data.title: Eli Lilly Q4 2024 Earnings Preview: All eyes on stronger 2025
data.description:
Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) is scheduled to announce Q4 earnings results on Thursday, February 6th, before market open.

The consensus EPS Estimate is $5.11 (+105.2% Y/Y) and the consensus Revenue Estimate is $13.42B (+43.5% Y/Y).

JP Morgan expects Q4 sales to fall below the consensus and full-year sales at the low end of the company’s guidance range. It views 2025 guidance more positively, driven by GLP blockbusters Mounjaro and Zepbound.

Eli Lilly is set to continue ramping up supply of the drugs in 2025 and benefit from broader label/coverage for Zepbound. Late-stage trial data on its next novel anti-obesity GLP-1 candidate, orforglipron, will also be a catalyst for investors.

Recent SA analyses have been largely bullish on the drugmaker, noting demand for key drugs Zepbound and Mounjaro remains strong, despite recent sales guidance cuts, and supply chain factors seem to be one of the few significant near-term headwinds.

Continued production increases, new product launches and pipeline development will remain the big drivers for 2025.

Over the last 2 years, LLY has beaten EPS estimates 63% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 63% of the time.

Over the last 3 months, EPS estimates have seen 2 upward revisions and 12 downward. Revenue estimates have seen 0 upward revisions and 10 downward.

data.title: Landmark Bancorp sees 20% annualized loan growth with expanding margins in Q4 2024
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Landmark Bancorp, Inc. (NASDAQ:LARK) Q4 2024

Management View

  • Abby Wendel, President and CEO, reported solid earnings for Q4 2024 with net income of $3.3 million, driven by significant loan growth and higher net interest income. She highlighted a 20% annualized increase in total gross loans this quarter, reaching nearly $1.1 billion. Deposit balances rose by $53 million, contributing to reduced short-term borrowings and improved net interest margin.
  • Wendel noted, “Diluted earnings per share this quarter totaled $0.57, an increase of 25% over the same quarter last year.”
  • CFO Mark Herpich stated, “Net interest income for the fourth quarter totaled $12.4 million, an increase of $795,000 compared to the third quarter.” He emphasized the effect of lower interest expense on deposits and borrowings, along with the Federal Reserve’s rate cuts, on bolstering the margin.
  • Chief Credit Officer Raymond McLanahan outlined loan portfolio growth across multiple categories, with increases in commercial real estate, commercial, agricultural, and residential mortgage loans. Gross loans outstanding reached a record $1.052 billion.

Outlook

  • Management expressed confidence in further margin expansion throughout 2025, even in a stable rate environment. Wendel indicated that “other levers” on the deposit side will also support margin improvement.
  • Wendel projected continued focus on enhancing infrastructure and leveraging relationship-based banking to grow the customer base. She emphasized efforts to maintain credit discipline and adapt to the evolving rate environment.
  • McLanahan highlighted that approximately 30% of the loan portfolio is expected to reprice in 2025, positioning the bank to withstand potential rate changes.

Financial Results

  • Q4 2024 net income totaled $3.3 million, compared to $3.9 million in Q3 2024. The decline was attributed to lower non-interest income, higher non-interest expense, and a $1.5 million provision for credit losses.
  • Net interest margin increased to 3.51%, up 21 basis points from the prior quarter. Interest expense on deposits decreased by $480,000, while interest on borrowed funds dropped by $363,000.
  • Non-interest income declined by $882,000 from Q3 2024 due to a $1 million securities loss and reduced residential mortgage sales. However, an increase of $722,000 in bank-owned life insurance income partially offset these declines.
  • Non-interest expense rose by $1.3 million quarter-over-quarter, driven by higher professional fees and compensation costs.
  • The allowance for credit losses totaled $12.8 million, representing 1.22% of gross loans, with net charge-offs of $219,000 during the quarter.

Q&A

  • Ross Haberman, RLH Investments, asked about the impact of loan repricing and the potential for margin expansion in 2025. Raymond McLanahan responded that while rates are difficult to predict, the portfolio is expected to follow rate trends, and Wendel added that deposit-side strategies would also support margin growth.
  • Haberman inquired about the sustainability of loan growth. Wendel attributed the strong pipeline to the efforts of the commercial banking team but noted that achieving 20% annualized growth every quarter might not be necessary.
  • John Rodis, Janney, questioned strategic priorities for 2025. Wendel highlighted a focus on improving associate and customer experiences, investing in infrastructure, and exploring M&A opportunities to expand Landmark’s footprint in Kansas.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts showed cautious optimism, focusing on sustainability of loan growth and margin expansion. Haberman’s questions reflected curiosity about future growth drivers, while Rodis sought clarity on strategic direction.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing discipline and adaptability. Wendel stated, “We are well positioned to withstand potential changes in the rate environment.”
  • Compared to Q3 2024, management’s tone remained consistent, with continued emphasis on growth and operational improvements.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Net income declined from $3.9 million in Q3 to $3.3 million in Q4 2024 due to higher expenses and increased credit loss provisions.
  • Loan growth accelerated to $50.5 million in Q4, compared to $21.3 million in Q3. Non-interest income, however, declined by $882,000 after a $533,000 increase in the prior quarter.
  • Management tone and strategic priorities remained focused on disciplined growth and infrastructure investment.
  • Analysts’ tone shifted slightly, with more emphasis on understanding loan growth sustainability and margin dynamics.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management flagged a $1.5 million provision for credit losses tied to strong loan growth and one non-accrual loan. Net charge-offs increased to $219,000.
  • Declines in non-interest income, including a $1 million securities loss, and higher non-interest expenses were identified as challenges.
  • Analysts raised concerns about the repeatability of strong loan growth and the impact of rate stability on margins.

Final Takeaway

Landmark Bancorp reported strong Q4 2024 results, highlighted by robust loan growth, expanding net interest margin, and disciplined expense management. The company remains confident in its ability to sustain growth through 2025, leveraging its relationship-based banking model and infrastructure investments. Analysts showed interest in margin trends and loan growth sustainability, while management emphasized resilience and adaptability in navigating evolving rate dynamics.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Simon Property upgraded to Overweight at Piper after mixed Q4 results
data.description:
Piper Sandler upgraded Simon Property Group (NYSE:SPG) to Overweight from Neutral as the shopping mall REIT's 2025 outlook highlights the underlying earnings power of its real estate portfolio.

The company is contemplating ways to further maximize its investments in retailers and brands, which could lead to an exit, analyst Alexander Goldfarb said in his note. That returns the spotlight to growing opportunities in the REIT's real estate portfolio, he added.

Simon Property (NYSE:SPG) stock rose 3.0% in Wednesday afternoon trading after the company turned in an earnings miss and revenue beat and issued 2025 guidance with a midpoint slightly under the consensus.

"Management seems increasingly comfortable throttling up on the external front, which looks to accelerate cash flow," Goldfarb wrote. "Thus, our concerns about slowing earnings have been allayed."

Piper's Overweight rating on Simon Property (NYSE:SPG) contrasts with the SA Quant rating of Hold and aligns with the average SA Analyst and average Wall Street ratings of Buy.

data.title: Finning outlines momentum with $2.6B backlog and strategic growth in South America and power systems
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Finning International Inc. (OTCPK:FINGF) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Kevin Parkes highlighted a record performance in 2024, with net revenues growing by 6% year-over-year to $10.1 billion. He attributed the growth to strengthened product support capabilities and equipment sales, which saw increases of over 10% for new equipment and nearly 30% for used equipment.
  • The product support revenue reached a record $5.5 billion, with notable growth in South America, where functional currency revenue rose by 6%. The company also added nearly 350 technicians in the region to meet demand.
  • Parkes emphasized a $550 million backlog increase in 2024, driven by partnerships with Caterpillar and diverse end markets such as mining and power systems. The UK and Ireland power systems backlog grew by 150% due to multiyear data center projects.
  • CFO Greg Palaschuk reported Q4 net revenue of $2.6 billion, marking a 7% growth year-over-year, with product support revenue up 6%. Adjusted EPS reached $1.02, a 7% increase from Q4 2023, supported by cost controls and a reduced share count.

Outlook

  • Management noted optimism for 2025, particularly in South America, driven by growing mining activity and autonomous fleet expansions. The Chilean copper sector remains strong, supported by steady infrastructure construction and mining contractor demand.
  • In Canada, Parkes expressed cautious optimism amid improving oil sands activity, though construction remains slow. He expects stable demand for product support but anticipates softness in construction through the winter months.
  • The UK and Ireland are expected to see continued growth in power systems and used equipment, despite a challenging economic environment. Data center projects are expected to contribute significantly to future revenue.
  • Parkes reiterated a focus on maximizing product support growth, particularly in South America and Canada, as part of the company's 2025 priorities.

Financial Results

  • Q4 product support revenue grew 6% year-over-year, with South America leading the increase at 10%. The UK and Ireland saw a 5% rise in functional currency, while product support in Canada remained stable.
  • New equipment sales were up 12% year-over-year, driven by strong mining deliveries in South America and power systems demand in the UK and Ireland. However, rental revenue declined by 14% due to lower fleet utilization.
  • SG&A as a percentage of net revenue reached a record low of 16.3%, reflecting strong cost management efforts. Free cash flow stood at nearly $400 million for Q4, contributing to a full-year total of $865 million.
  • EBIT for Q4 was 10.9% in South America, 8.1% in Canada, and 5.8% in the UK and Ireland. Margins were impacted by a higher mix of low-margin mining equipment deliveries.

Q&A

  • Sabahat Khan, RBC: Asked about margin expectations in 2025 and the mix of product support. CFO Greg Palaschuk noted a focus on cost controls, highlighting strong South American growth and stabilization in Canada despite mixed activities.
  • Steve Hansen, Raymond James: Inquired about the capital improvement timeline. Palaschuk emphasized ongoing progress with a target to improve working capital velocity and achieve sustainable cost efficiency.
  • Yuri Lynk, Canaccord Genuity: Questioned South America's oil and gas activity sustainability. CEO Kevin Parkes pointed to significant opportunities in Argentina's Vaca Muerta basin, supported by the new government's policies.
  • Cherilyn Radbourne, TD Cowen: Asked about Caterpillar's $28 billion product support target. Parkes confirmed close collaboration with Caterpillar to capitalize on this goal.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on concerns around Canadian mining activity and construction softness. The tone was mixed but leaned toward positive as management demonstrated confidence in addressing challenges.
  • Management maintained a positive tone, particularly when discussing South America's growth and power systems opportunities in all regions. However, they acknowledged near-term uncertainties in Canada and the UK.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management appeared more confident in South America's prospects but maintained a cautious outlook for Canada and construction-related segments.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q4 saw stronger product support growth in South America (10% vs. 7% in Q3) and an improved backlog of $2.6 billion compared to $2.3 billion in Q3. However, rental revenue declined further in Q4 (-14% vs. -12% in Q3).
  • Adjusted EPS of $1.02 in Q4 marked a sequential improvement over Q3’s $0.94. Free cash flow also increased significantly to nearly $400 million in Q4, compared to $346 million in Q3.
  • Management's tone showed greater confidence in Q4, driven by record revenues and backlog levels, while still emphasizing cost control and operational efficiency.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted challenges in attracting and retaining qualified labor, particularly in South America, to meet growing demand.
  • Canadian mining customers' spending discipline and deferred maintenance continue to pose risks to product support growth.
  • The rental business remains under pressure, with utilization levels below expectations due to construction market softness in Canada.

Final Takeaway

Finning International demonstrated strong performance in Q4 2024, achieving record revenues and product support growth despite challenges in Canada and rental markets. The company enters 2025 with a $2.6 billion backlog and a clear focus on capitalizing on growth opportunities in South America and power systems. Management's strategic execution and cost discipline position the company for continued resilience and long-term growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Evercore projects continued growth with robust M&A pipeline and 2024 revenue of $3B
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Evercore Inc. (NYSE:EVR) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO John Weinberg highlighted that 2024 was Evercore's second-best year for revenue, reaching over $3 billion in adjusted net revenues, with strength across all business segments. He noted the firm advised on three of the seven largest global M&A deals and observed further diversification with over 40% of revenues derived from non-M&A sources.
  • Weinberg emphasized optimism for 2025, citing improving market trends, strong backlogs, and increased CEO confidence levels. He announced Evercore's role as lead financial adviser on Calpine's $29.1 billion sale to Constellation Energy, the largest announced deal this year.
  • CFO Tim LaLonde reported adjusted earnings per share of $3.41 for Q4, marking a 69% increase year-over-year, and a full-year adjusted EPS of $9.42, up 46% from 2023. He highlighted a 280 basis point improvement in adjusted operating margin for the year, reaching 18.6%.

Outlook

  • Management expressed confidence in a gradual recovery in the deal-making environment throughout 2025, supported by a strong macroeconomic backdrop and robust client activity.
  • Weinberg noted ongoing strength in financial sponsors activity, including liability management and equity underwriting, and highlighted Evercore's expanded capabilities in structured capital solutions and GP/LP advisory services.
  • The firm anticipates a stronger IPO market in 2025, with continued growth in equity capital markets activity driven by an increasingly diversified sector exposure.

Financial Results

  • Adjusted net revenues for Q4 totaled $981 million, a 24% increase year-over-year. Full-year adjusted revenues of $3 billion were up 23% from 2023.
  • Adjusted advisory fees for Q4 reached $850 million, up 29% year-over-year, making it the firm's second-strongest quarter on record. Full-year advisory fees totaled $2.4 billion, a 24% improvement from last year.
  • Underwriting revenues for 2024 rose 41% to $157 million, while commissions and related revenues grew 6%, reaching $214 million for the year.
  • Wealth management achieved record revenues and assets under management of $13.9 billion by year-end.

Q&A

  • Mike Brown, Wells Fargo: Asked about comp ratio improvements and expectations for 2025. CFO Tim LaLonde emphasized a commitment to meaningful improvement while balancing strategic investments in talent.
  • Brennan Hawken, UBS: Inquired about recruiting and retention efforts. CEO Weinberg highlighted the addition of 60 senior managing directors in the past three years, with ongoing efforts to attract high-quality talent.
  • Devin Ryan, Citizens JMP: Questioned the M&A pipeline and large-deal dynamics. Weinberg noted robust activity levels, supported by strong client dialogues and a gradual regulatory loosening.
  • Aidan Hall, KBW: Asked about competitive positioning in private capital advisory. Weinberg pointed to record results in 2024 and optimism for sustained growth, driven by new products and expanded client relationships.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were cautiously optimistic, focusing on questions about cost management, market recovery timelines, and competitive positioning. Concerns centered on potential regulatory impacts and the pace of sponsor activity recovery.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing strong performance metrics and a positive outlook. Phrases like "we are optimistic" and "we believe" reflected their confidence in future growth.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management demonstrated greater clarity and optimism, especially regarding backlog strength and market trends.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q4 revenues of $981 million showed a substantial increase from Q3's $740 million, reflecting higher advisory and underwriting fees.
  • Adjusted EPS rose from $2.04 in Q3 to $3.41 in Q4, driven by improved operating margins and revenue growth.
  • Management's tone remained optimistic but showed increased confidence in market recovery timelines and sponsor activity compared to the prior quarter.
  • Analysts' focus shifted from geopolitical uncertainties in Q3 to questions about competitive positioning and cost management in Q4.

Risks and Concerns

  • Weinberg acknowledged geopolitical and macroeconomic uncertainties as potential challenges to the deal-making environment.
  • Analysts raised concerns about competitive pressures in private capital advisory and the pace of recovery in sponsor-related activity.
  • Management addressed these concerns by highlighting strong client relationships, ongoing investments in talent, and a diversified revenue base.

Final Takeaway

Evercore's Q4 2024 results showcased significant revenue growth and improved margins, highlighting strength across advisory, underwriting, and wealth management segments. With a robust M&A pipeline, ongoing investments in talent, and optimism for improving market trends, the firm appears well-positioned for continued momentum in 2025. Management's confidence in strategic initiatives and a gradual recovery in the deal-making environment underscores Evercore's readiness to capitalize on emerging opportunities.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Cirrus Logic rises after Benchmark upgrades on 'solid' Q3 performance
data.description:
Shares of Cirrus Logic (NASDAQ:CRUS) jumped about 6% on Wednesday after Benchmark upgraded the stock to Buy from Hold following the company's fiscal third-quarter 2025 results.

Analyst David Williams said they were upgrading the shares and setting a $125 price target after the company "turned in a solid" third-quarter performance, beating revenue and earnings by a wide margin, with a healthy upside to the fourth-quarter outlook.

The performance was highlighted by record gross margins and better-than-expected handset volume ending the calendar year. While the continued success with the company's largest customer, Apple (AAPL), and ongoing content expansion opportunities are encouraging, the upgrade is driven by the continued customer and end market diversification progress, the analyst added.

Specifically, Williams sees multiple near-and-long-term growth vectors developing beyond its core smartphone business with accelerating momentum in the largely greenfield (and expanded), laptop market, and solid traction for its new timing products in Automotive and Professional Audio applications.

The analyst added that they do not see an immediate catalyst to drive shares appreciably higher in the short term, but do think that the improving end market mix will help smooth seasonal/customer volatility and drive sustainable, accretive, long-term revenue growth and earnings leverage.

Related stocks: Shares of Silicon Laboratories (SLAB) surged +9%, Allegro MicroSystems (ALGM) +5%, while MACOM Technology Solutions (MTSI) and Tower Semiconductor (TSEM) rose about +2%, each, on Wednesday.

data.title: Johnson Controls tops S&P 500 leaderboard after earnings beat and raise, new CEO hire
data.description:
Johnson Controls (NYSE:JCI) +12% in Wednesday's trading to lead the day's gainers on the S&P 500 after reporting Q1 adjusted earnings and revenues that exceeded Wall Street expectations and issuing above-consensus guidance for Q2 and FY 2025 earnings, while also naming a new CEO.

Q1 GAAP operating earnings jumped to $363M, or $0.55/share, from $$340M, or $0.50/share, in the year-earlier quarter, and revenues rose 4.2% Y/Y to $5.43B, as organic Q1 orders increased 16% Y/Y and the company's organic building solutions backlog rose 11% to $13.2B.

Johnson Controls (NYSE:JCI) issued upside guidance for Q2, seeing adjusted earnings of $0.77-$0.79/share, above $0.73 analyst consensus estimate, including sales growth of mid-single digits and adjusted segment EBITA margin of ~16.5%.

For FY 2025, the company raised guidance for earnings to $3.50-$3.60/share, up from its prior outlook for $3.40-$3.50 and above $3.47 consensus, including unchanged guidance for organic sales growth of mid-single digits and adjusted segment EBITA margin improvement of more than 80 bps Y/Y, up from previous guidance of more than 50 bps.

Johnson Controls (JCI) also announced that former Danaher executive Joakim Weidemanis will become its new CEO effective March 12, succeeding George Oliver.
